svg.title: The cascade from chronic stress to the skin in dogs — clinical diagram by Dr. Jessica Camacho García
svg.desc: Pathophysiological cascade: chronic stress and separation anxiety activate the HPA axis and raise cortisol persistently; sustained cortisol drives immunosuppression; this opens the door to opportunistic infections (adult-onset demodicosis, Malassezia, pyoderma) and endocrine mimics (pseudo-Cushing and sick euthyroid syndrome / NTIS); skin signs —licking, alopecia, relapsing infection— are the late warning. Clinical content by Dr. Jessica Ysabel Camacho García, CMVP 12434, ORCID 0009-0002-6837-5311. Design © Carlos Eduardo Ravello Joo.

cc:Work.dc:description: Diagram of the chronic-stress-to-skin cascade in dogs. Clinical author: Dr. Jessica Ysabel Camacho García (CMVP 12434, ORCID 0009-0002-6837-5311). Design: Carlos Eduardo Ravello Joo.

svg.text: The cascade from chronic stress to the skin
Why licking and hair loss are the last link, not the first
2 · HPA axis activated → sustained cortisol that won't drop
accumulates and can be measured in hair cortisol
= the LATE WARNING of a process months or years long
“The skin does not create the disease. It announces it.”
Clinical content: Dr. Jessica Ysabel Camacho García, DVM · CMVP 12434 · ORCID 0009-0002-6837-5311
